sessions.details: Recorded 1981-04-01. First TX 10 April 1981. No known commercial release.
Recorded 1970-02-04. First TX 1970-03-07 on BBC World Service Rhythm & Blues show. No known commercial release.

1971 Session. The track represented here (introduced by Brian Matthew) was recorded for the BBC three times on two different shows in that year, so at this stage it is not possible to tell which it is from.

track_listing.details: BBC World Service Rhythm & Blues Session
BBC World Service Rhythm & Blues Session

transcript.text: Also in stereo on VHF, this is National Radio 1.
Well hello there, this is TV on the Radio here, Thomas the Vance, and welcome once again to The Friday Rock Show.
Right, this pleasant summer's evening we have the repeat of our recent session by Zero, it's a good one.
And some archived material by the Groundhogs, as recorded in 1970 and 1971, including a live version of Split.
We've got some new releases by Paul Inder, Bernie Marsden,
Barclay James Harvest, Gillan, Van Halen, and Iron Maiden.
Classics by Brand X, Eversmith, Wishbone Ash,
Eric Clapton, and the Pink Floyd, among others.
Not forgetting our Friday night connection.
And tonight, we start with this.
